title: Air-fryer chicken thighs
subtitle: Smoked paprika chicken with crisp skin
excerpt: Bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs seasoned with smoked paprika, garlic, and black pepper, then cooked in the air fryer until the skin is lacquered and deeply golden. Faster than the oven, crispier than a pan. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Bone-in chicken thighs are the most forgiving cut in the air fryer. The fat renders out through the skin during cooking, self-basting the meat and providing the fat the fan circulates into a crust. The smoked paprika seasoning draws on the Spanish pimentón tradition of using dried red pepper as a coating for grilled and roasted meats.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Season the chicken up to 24 hours ahead and refrigerate uncovered so the skin dries out for better crisping.

content.text: Chicken thighs are the best cut for the air fryer. The bone conducts heat to the centre while the skin renders and crisps. The result is a thigh with more crunch than you can get from a conventional oven in twice the time. Pat the skin completely dry before seasoning: moisture is the enemy of a crust.

Pat the chicken thighs completely dry with kitchen paper. Mix together {{paprika-smoked}} of smoked paprika, {{garlic-powder}} of garlic powder, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt, and {{black-pepper}} of black pepper. Rub {{olive-oil}} of olive oil over the skin, then coat all over with the spice mix, pressing it into the skin. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Place the thighs skin-side up in the basket. Cook for 22–25 minutes until the skin is dark golden and the juices run clear at the thigh. Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.symptom: Skin pale and soft rather than golden and crisp
items.cause: Skin not dried before seasoning, or air fryer not preheated.
items.fix: Pat the skin dry with kitchen paper until there is no visible moisture. Preheat the air fryer before the chicken goes in — cold air doesn't crisp.

items.symptom: Chicken cooked through but skin not crisp
items.fix: Increase to 210°C for the last 3 minutes. If the meat is already at temperature, keep going with just the skin-side blast.

items.cause: Fat dripping onto the heating element as it renders from the skin.
items.fix: Add 2 tablespoons of water to the base of the air fryer drawer (not the basket) before cooking. This catches drips and prevents smoking.

content.text: Leaving the skin on is the right choice here. The fat under the skin renders into the circulating air and bastes the meat as it cooks. Remove the skin before cooking and the thigh will be drier and less flavourful, and you lose the whole point of the technique. If you prefer to eat without skin, cook it on and peel it off at the table.
